live_message: Daniel Brooks is on a call right now. The agent is qualifying him — custody modification, hearing in three weeks.
live_message_es: Daniel Brooks está en una llamada ahora mismo. El agente lo está calificando — modificación de custodia, audiencia en tres semanas.

calls.name: Daniel Brooks
calls.summary: Currently on call — custody modification consult

transcript.text: Garner Family Law, intake line. How can I help?
I need to talk to a lawyer about custody. My ex is moving to North Carolina and wants to take my son.
I'm sorry you're dealing with that. Let me get you set up with an attorney consult. Are you the parent currently with primary custody?
We share fifty fifty. Court order is from twenty twenty-three.
Got it. Is there a hearing date set or anything time-sensitive?
She filed the relocation motion last week. Hearing's October seventeenth, so about three weeks.

calls.summary_long: Daniel Brooks calling about a custody modification — co-parent has filed for relocation to North Carolina, hearing scheduled October 17th (three weeks out). Existing 50/50 custody order from 2023. Time-sensitive matter — will route to senior attorney for same-week consult. Call in progress.

calls.name: Anonymous caller
calls.summary: Asked about consult fees + payment plans

calls.name: Rebecca Hayes

transcript.text: Garner Family Law, how can I help?
Hi, I'm thinking about filing for divorce. I don't know where to start.
Many people start exactly here. We do a one-hour confidential consult — $250, applied toward your retainer if you move forward. May I get your name?
Rebecca Hayes. We've been separated for about four months.
Are children involved, Rebecca?
Two. Eight and eleven.
Thank you. I have Thursday 3:00 PM or Monday 10:00 AM open. Which works?
Thursday three o'clock please.

Booked. Thursday 3:00 PM, one-hour confidential intake with Ms. Garner. I'll text you our pre-consult checklist — bring whatever financial documents you have. Everything stays privileged from this moment forward.

calls.summary_long: Rebecca Hayes called to start divorce proceedings. Separated 4 months, two children (ages 8 and 11). Booked Thursday 3:00 PM with Ms. Garner (60m, $250 consult fee applied to retainer). Pre-consult checklist SMS'd. No urgent safety concerns disclosed.

calls.name: Anonymous caller
calls.summary: After-hours — emergency PFA question (transferred to attorney pager)

transcript.text: Garner Family Law, after-hours line. How can I help?
I think I need a PFA. My husband just left and I'm scared he's coming back.
I hear you. Are you safe right now? Is anyone with you?
Yes. My sister's here.

Good. I'm going to do two things right now. First, if you're in immediate danger, please call 911. Second, I'm paging our on-call attorney — she'll call you back within fifteen minutes to walk you through emergency PFA options. What's the best number to reach you?
This number.
Got it. Ms. Reilly will call you back from a 610 number in the next fifteen minutes. Stay where you're safe.

calls.summary_long: Anonymous after-hours call — caller fearing for safety, asking about emergency PFA (Protection From Abuse order). Verified caller is currently safe with sister present. Paged on-call attorney Ms. Reilly with 15-minute callback promise. Sensitive matter — no scheduling action taken at this stage; live attorney handoff.

calls.name: Anonymous caller
calls.summary: Asked about criminal defense (referred out — not a fit)
